jobs.label: analyze_and_plan
jobs.prompt:
ROADMAP: roadmap.break.up.provider.onboarding.py.3.074.line.god.module

Analyze provider_onboarding.py and produce a split plan.

Read Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/provider_onboarding.py (it's ~3,074 lines).

OBJECTIVE: Do NOT make code changes yet. Produce a split plan:
1. Identify natural split boundaries (detection, probing, registration, CLI, wizard flow, etc.)
2. Propose 4-6 focused modules with names and responsibilities
3. Identify shared utilities that should go in a new provider_onboarding_utils.py
4. Note any circular dependencies the split must avoid
5. Estimate which split would have the most impact if done first

Submit the plan via praxis_submit_research_result.

jobs.label: execute_split
jobs.prompt:
ROADMAP: roadmap.break.up.provider.onboarding.py.3.074.line.god.module

Execute the provider_onboarding.py split based on the analysis above.

The split plan from the previous job is in your context. Implement it.

OBJECTIVE: Split into focused modules. Minimum viable split:
- runtime/provider_detection.py — model/provider detection logic
- runtime/provider_probe.py — smoke probing and validation
- runtime/provider_registry_writer.py — DB writes for model registration
- provider_onboarding.py becomes a thin orchestrator importing from these

Keep the public interface of provider_onboarding.py intact — no breaking changes to callers.

Run existing tests to confirm nothing broke.

Submit code change summary via praxis_submit_code_change.

jobs.label: verify_split
jobs.prompt:
ROADMAP: roadmap.break.up.provider.onboarding.py.3.074.line.god.module

Verify the provider_onboarding.py split.

Read the newly split modules and the updated provider_onboarding.py.

Check:
1. No circular imports between the split modules
2. provider_onboarding.py is now significantly smaller (target: < 500 lines)
3. Each new module has a clear, single responsibility
4. Existing callers still work (imports unchanged)
5. Run tests: PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/ -k 'provider'

Submit code change summary or list of issues via praxis_submit_code_change.
